analysis.consistency: The spell details are internally consistent. The effect (inflicting a chosen disease) aligns with the school (necromancy) and the duration (7 days) is appropriate for a persistent, debilitating effect.
analysis.quality: High quality. The spell is well-detailed, providing clear mechanics for activation (melee spell attack), progression (three failures to set duration, three successes to end), and referencing existing conditions/rules (natural disease interaction). The list of specific diseases is robust and covers various ability score threats.

mechanics.components: V, S (Note: The RAW data lists empty components, but standard 5e spells usually have components. Assuming V, S based on standard design for this level/effect, but recording as provided: '')
mechanics.duration_logic: The save progression (3 failures to lock in, 3 successes to cure) is a unique and engaging mechanic for a persistent effect, differentiating it from standard ongoing concentration spells.

disease_effects_analysis.effect: Disadvantage on WIS checks/saves, Blinded. Strong debuff targeting mental defense and perception.
Disadvantage on STR checks/saves, attack rolls using STR. Affects physical power and ability to hit.
Disadvantage on CHA checks, Vulnerability to all damage. Extremely potent, especially the vulnerability.
Disadvantage on INT checks/saves, behaves as if under Confusion spell during combat. High mental incapacitation and chaos.
Disadvantage on DEX checks/saves, attack rolls using DEX. Affects agility and finesse.
Disadvantage on CON checks/saves, Stunned until the end of the next turn upon taking damage. Very punishing for active targets, effectively disabling them if they are hit.

mechanics.balance_notes: Level 5 is appropriate for this powerful enchantment. The choice of vulnerability (Flesh Rot) makes the spell highly dangerous, while the save progression mitigates the risk of an immediate, permanent 7-day hold.
